{"society_power": 1.3, "state_power": 7.6, "society_change": 0.6, "state_change": 0.3, "key_event": "Postwar boom, urbanization, and first legal African associations", "reasoning": "The mining/uranium boom and welfare colonialism further strengthened an already-dominant despotic state, though it neared a fiscal/administrative ceiling. Legalized trade unions (1946), the \u00e9volu\u00e9 class, and cultural associations like ABAKO (1950) quietly raised society's latent organizational capacity from a very suppressed base, still tightly caged and without political rights."}
